steps.title: Identify where Odom places blame
steps.content: Odom directly identifies the source of bias: researchers concentrated their studies “near universities in the temperate northern hemisphere, where female birdsong is less common”. Geographic site selection—not deliberate male-bird preference—caused the mischaracterization.

steps.title: Match Odom's explanation to the answer
steps.content: D directly states Odom's reasoning: the bias was “influenced by the kinds of study sites researchers tended to select”. A, B, and C invoke unrelated causes (deliberate subject selection, evolution, behavioral differences). That matches choice D — D is correct.
